task_completion.description: Verify that Stellarium is set to Chelyabinsk on February 15, 2013 at the correct time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom status bar in Stellarium should display 'Chelyabinsk' (or coordinates ~55°09'N 61°24'E) and the date '2013-02-15'. The time should be 03:20:00 (if in UTC) or 09:20:00 (if in local Chelyabinsk time, UTC+6).

task_completion.description: Verify that Atmosphere, Azimuthal Grid, and Cardinal Points are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky should appear bright/blue indicating morning glare (Atmosphere enabled). There should be curved, radiating grid lines across the sky (Azimuthal Grid enabled). Red compass letters like N, S, E, W should be visible along the horizon (Cardinal Points enabled).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Sun is centered in the viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun must be clearly visible and positioned in the center of the Stellarium sky view.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window showing the '~/Pictures/stellarium' directory containing a screenshot image file, or an on-screen Stellarium notification confirming the screenshot was saved.

task_completion.description: Verify the incident analysis note was created with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the file 'chelyabinsk_glare_analysis.txt' on the Desktop. The text must explain the meteor's trajectory aligning with the solar glare and explicitly include the words 'Chelyabinsk', '2013', and either 'Sun' or 'solar'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Stellarium GUI to render the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Stellarium application window must be visibly open and rendering the sky scene, rather than the agent solely relying on terminal commands to edit configuration files.

integrity.description: Ensure the analysis note is an original explanation and not a copy-paste of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the editor should read as a coherent, natural explanation of the event, rather than a direct copy-paste of the task instructions.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't just use echo commands to bypass opening a text editor for the analysis note.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or nano/vim in a terminal) actively displaying the contents of the text file, proving manual or simulated manual entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is Chelyabinsk, Russia (55.15° N, 61.40° E, 210m). The target date is 2013-02-15. The target time is 03:20:00 UTC (which corresponds to 09:20:00 local time in Chelyabinsk, UTC+6). The required display flags are: Atmosphere (makes the sky bright), Azimuthal Grid (adds circular grid lines), and Cardinal Points (adds N, S, E, W labels). The analysis note must be saved to ~/Desktop/chelyabinsk_glare_analysis.txt and must contain the words 'Chelyabinsk', '2013', and either 'Sun' or 'solar'.
